text.content:
When this set is chosen, at the start of the show (or on the first turn of a Vampyre during initiative), music begins to play; all non-Vampyre actors in the scene without Level Damper cyberware are completely deafened until the music is shut off/the Vampyres defeated. Additionally, the highest ranking Vampyre in the scene (if there are multiple, choose one) goes Center Stage.

While on center stage, the Vampyre is putting on some kind of performance, and performs as an action on each of their turns in place of any other actions. After doing so, distribute luck points to Vamypres in the scene - for every 4 Vampyres, distribute 1 point.

If the center stage performer is killed, incapacitated, or surrenders, the remaining Vampyres lose access to their gang-wide ability until a new one can be chosen and begins to perform.

Mook Options:

  • 1 Point, during the Vampyre's turn: Hyped up by the performance, they ignore negative roll penalties during their turn, and gain +3 to any checks that are not suffering roll penalties.

  • 2 Points, during another Vampyre's turn: Synchronizing their movements, the Vampyre using this ability may immediately take the same action as their compatriot has just done, assuming it's possible for them to do so (if it's a dash action, the emulating mook must move in the same direction, as close to the same amount of spaces as possible, and cannot otherwise move).

  • 3 Points, at the start of any turn: The performance reaches a crescendo, dazzling and stunning everyone present to see it. Every non-Vampyre in the scene must make a DV15 WILL Endurance check; on failure, they suffer the Damaged Eye and Damaged Ear critical injuries until the end of their next turn. If the check is failed by 5 or more, they are suppressed, with the center stage performer acting as the source of the effect. Cannot be triggered more than once a round.
